US Postal Tracking › Reno, NV › Attempted Delivery

USPS "Attempted Delivery" in Reno, NV: How to Reschedule

Your USPS package is had a failed delivery attempt in Reno, NV. Real-time tracking updates and next steps for Reno (Southwest) area packages.

What Does "Attempted Delivery" Mean for Reno Packages?

When USPS tracking shows "Attempted Delivery" in Reno, NV, your package is had a failed delivery attempt. The Reno USPS network in the Southwest region serves 264,165 residents with 2 postal facilities processing approximately 211K packages daily. This status is one of the most common tracking updates seen by Reno residents, and understanding what it means can help reduce anxiety about your shipment.

How Long Does "Attempted Delivery" Last in Reno?

For packages in the Southwest region, the "Attempted Delivery" status typically updates within 24 to 72 hours. However, during peak shipping seasons such as November through January, processing times at the Reno Main Post Office can be longer due to increased volume. Packages shipped via Priority Mail usually move through this status faster than those sent via Ground Advantage or Parcel Select. If your package has been showing "Attempted Delivery" for more than 5 business days, consider contacting USPS directly.

What to Do When Your Package Shows "Attempted Delivery"

Here are the recommended steps for Reno residents when their package shows this status:

  1. Wait 24-48 hours — Tracking updates are not always instantaneous, especially during busy periods at the Reno Main Post Office.
  2. Check tracking online — Visit US Postal Tracking and enter your tracking number for the most current status.
  3. Sign up for notifications — USPS Informed Delivery sends automatic email and text alerts for packages headed to your Reno address.
  4. Contact your local post office — The Reno Main Post Office can provide additional details about packages in their system.
  5. Call USPS — Reach USPS Customer Service at 1-800-ASK-USPS (1-800-275-8777), Monday-Friday 8 AM to 8:30 PM ET.
  6. File a search request — After 7+ business days without updates, file a Missing Mail search at usps.com/help/missing-mail.htm.

Reno USPS Post Office Information

Main FacilityReno Main Post Office
Facilities2 USPS locations
Daily Volume211K packages
ZIP Codes Served89501, 89502, 89503, 89504
Standard HoursMon-Fri: 8:30 AM – 5:00 PM | Sat: 9:00 AM – 12:00 PM
RegionSouthwest
Population264,165 residents
Customer Service1-800-ASK-USPS (1-800-275-8777)

USPS Delivery Times to Reno, NV

Estimated USPS delivery times for packages shipped to Reno vary by service type:

ServiceEstimated Time
Priority Mail Express1-2 business days
Priority Mail1-3 business days
First-Class Mail1-5 business days
Ground Advantage2-5 business days
Media Mail2-8 business days

These times are estimates and may vary based on origin location, weather conditions, and current USPS processing volumes in the Southwest region.

Landmarks Near Post Offices in Reno

Key landmarks in the Reno delivery area include National Automobile Museum, Nevada Museum of Art. Packages addressed to locations near these landmarks are routed through the Reno Main Post Office, which serves as the central processing hub for the Reno metro area.

Frequently Asked Questions

What does "Attempted Delivery" mean for packages in Reno, NV?

When USPS shows "Attempted Delivery" in Reno, NV, it means your package is had a failed delivery attempt. The Reno Main Post Office processes approximately 211K packages daily, so brief delays during peak times are normal.

How long does "Attempted Delivery" last in Reno?

In the Southwest region, packages showing "Attempted Delivery" typically update within 24-72 hours. Reno has 2 USPS facilities serving ZIP codes 89501, 89502, 89503, 89504, and packages may pass through multiple facilities before delivery.

Where is the nearest post office in Reno, NV?

The main postal facility in Reno is the Reno Main Post Office. Reno has 2 USPS facilities throughout the city. Standard hours are Monday-Friday 8:30 AM – 5:00 PM and Saturday 9:00 AM – 12:00 PM.

Can I pick up my package at a Reno post office?

Yes. Bring a valid photo ID and your tracking number to the Reno Main Post Office or any USPS location in Reno. Packages are typically held for up to 15 days before being returned to the sender.

What should I do if my package is stuck on "Attempted Delivery" in Reno?

Wait 24-48 hours for updates. If there is no change, contact USPS at 1-800-275-8777 or visit the Reno Main Post Office in person. You can also file a Missing Mail search request at usps.com after 7 business days without an update.

Track Your Reno Package Now

Enter your USPS tracking number at US Postal Tracking for real-time updates on packages in the Reno, NV area. Our free tracking tool provides instant status updates for all USPS mail classes including Priority Mail, First-Class, Ground Advantage, and international shipments to and from ZIP codes 89501, 89502, 89503, 89504.
